mechanism_narrative: Elevated federal funds rates act as a persistent discount rate on future corporate cash flows, compressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Federal funds rate level shows no significant negative correlation with 20-day forward returns over any 3-year rolling window (Rank IC > -0.03).

mechanism_narrative: An unemployment rate exceeding 5.0% signals excess labor market slack that reduces household income growth and compresses corporate earnings expectations, driving negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 60-day returns in more than 50% of triggered instances despite the negative signal.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent month-over-month declines in industrial production and retail sales indicate a coordinated demand collapse across manufacturing and consumer sectors, leading to downward earnings revisions and negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The interaction term of INDPRO and RSAFS is not significant (p > 0.05) in predicting 20-day returns, or joint declines fail to produce more negative returns than either series alone.

mechanism_narrative: During monetary tightening cycles, an acceleration in consumer price inflation forces markets to reprice terminal policy rate expectations upward, expanding equity risk premiums and generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 60-day forward returns are not significantly lower during triggered tightening-cycle inflation accelerations compared to baseline neutral periods (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Sustained increases in industrial production reflect expanding manufacturing output and capacity utilization, which supports corporate revenue growth and margin improvement, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Industrial production growth shows no positive correlation with 20-day forward returns over any 3-year rolling window (Rank IC < 0.03), or positive returns occur in fewer than 55% of strong IP growth months.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp monthly decline in housing starts signals weakening residential investment and downstream demand for construction materials and consumer durables, prompting immediate risk-off repositioning and negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 posts positive 5-day returns in more than 50% of instances when housing starts drop more than 5% month-over-month.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent strength in nonfarm payrolls and retail sales establishes a self-reinforcing wage-income-consumption loop that boosts corporate top-line growth, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The interaction of PAYEMS and RSAFS does not add explanatory power beyond individual series (F-test for interaction term p > 0.05) over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A federal funds rate persistently below 2.0% signals that monetary policy is constrained by economic weakness rather than stimulative optimism, leading to negative risk asset repricing as recession probability dominates over growth recovery over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 delivers positive 60-day returns in more than 50% of observations when FEDFUNDS is below 2.0%, or the low-rate regime underperforms baseline (t-test p > 0.05).
